prompt: Which courses have the highest failure rate?

inputData.question: Which courses have the highest failure rate?

response.bodyRaw:
{"status":"success","steps":{"input":{"question":"Which courses have the highest failure rate?"},"sql":{"payload":{"question":"Which courses have the highest failure rate?"},"startedAt":1771855575018,"status":"success","output":{"reasoning":"The user wants to identify courses with the highest failure rates. Based on the schema exploration, I found that:
- grade_value is a custom enum type that needs to be cast to text then numeric for comparison
- A failure is defined as a grade below 3.0 (based on the grade values found: 2.0, 3.0, 3.5, 4.0, 4.5, 5.0)
- The query joins courses → course_sections → enrollments → grades
- Failure rate is calculated as: count of grades < 3.0 / total grades per course
- Results should be ordered by failure_rate DESC to show highest first

The successful test query confirmed the casting approach works: (g.grade_value)::text::NUMERIC
","sql":"
SELECT 
  c.code,
  c.name,
  ROUND((COUNT(CASE WHEN (g.grade_value)::text::NUMERIC < 3.0 THEN 1 END)::NUMERIC / COUNT(g.id)::NUMERIC)::NUMERIC, 4) AS failure_rate,
  COUNT(g.id) AS total_grades,
  COUNT(CASE WHEN (g.grade_value)::text::NUMERIC < 3.0 THEN 1 END) AS failed_count
FROM courses c
JOIN course_sections cs ON c.id = cs.course_id
JOIN enrollments e ON cs.id = e.course_section_id
JOIN grades g ON e.id = g.enrollment_id
WHERE g.grade_value IS NOT NULL
GROUP BY c.id, c.code, c.name
ORDER BY failure_rate DESC, c.code ASC
","assumptions":["Failure is defined as a grade value below 3.0","grade_value is a custom enum type requiring text-to-numeric casting","Only grades with non-null values are considered","Results are ordered by failure rate descending, then by course code for deterministic ordering"]},"endedAt":1771855647272},"run-raw-sql":{"payload":{"sql":"

SELECT 
  c.code,
  c.name,
  ROUND((COUNT(CASE WHEN (g.grade_value)::text::NUMERIC < 3.0 THEN 1 END)::NUMERIC / COUNT(g.id)::NUMERIC)::NUMERIC, 4) AS failure_rate,
  COUNT(g.id) AS total_grades,
  COUNT(CASE WHEN (g.grade_value)::text::NUMERIC < 3.0 THEN 1 END) AS failed_count
FROM courses c
JOIN course_sections cs ON c.id = cs.course_id
JOIN enrollments e ON cs.id = e.course_section_id
JOIN grades g ON e.id = g.enrollment_id
WHERE g.grade_value IS NOT NULL
GROUP BY c.id, c.code, c.name
ORDER BY failure_rate DESC, c.code ASC

resultRaw.sql:
SELECT 
  c.code,
  c.name,
  ROUND((COUNT(CASE WHEN (g.grade_value)::text::NUMERIC < 3.0 THEN 1 END)::NUMERIC / COUNT(g.id)::NUMERIC)::NUMERIC, 4) AS failure_rate,
  COUNT(g.id) AS total_grades,
  COUNT(CASE WHEN (g.grade_value)::text::NUMERIC < 3.0 THEN 1 END) AS failed_count
FROM courses c
JOIN course_sections cs ON c.id = cs.course_id
JOIN enrollments e ON cs.id = e.course_section_id
JOIN grades g ON e.id = g.enrollment_id
WHERE g.grade_value IS NOT NULL
GROUP BY c.id, c.code, c.name
ORDER BY failure_rate DESC, c.code ASC

result.sql:
SELECT 
  c.code,
  c.name,
  ROUND((COUNT(CASE WHEN (g.grade_value)::text::NUMERIC < 3.0 THEN 1 END)::NUMERIC / COUNT(g.id)::NUMERIC)::NUMERIC, 4) AS failure_rate,
  COUNT(g.id) AS total_grades,
  COUNT(CASE WHEN (g.grade_value)::text::NUMERIC < 3.0 THEN 1 END) AS failed_count
FROM courses c
JOIN course_sections cs ON c.id = cs.course_id
JOIN enrollments e ON cs.id = e.course_section_id
JOIN grades g ON e.id = g.enrollment_id
WHERE g.grade_value IS NOT NULL
GROUP BY c.id, c.code, c.name
ORDER BY failure_rate DESC, c.code ASC
